findings: Diff review (OWASP): both unstaged hunks change only literal text inside one template-literal message string each (keel-watch.cjs line 96 staleCheck HALTED warning; keel-state.cjs line 156 notifyHalt Slack text) — no control-flow change, no new sinks, no new inputs, no new interpolation added by the diff.

Injection: neither changed string reaches exec/eval/shell — one goes to process.stdout, the other into JSON.stringify() as the Slack POST body (safe JSON encoding). The '~/.keel/bin/keel-state.cjs' path in the messages is inert instructional text for a human, not code-resolved.

Sensitive-data check PASS: changed Slack message and stale warning contain story id, phase, attempt count, failure reasons, and the documented install path only — no credentials, tokens, or PII. The Slack webhook URL (the only secret in play) is read from ~/.keel/secrets/slack.webhook, used solely as the request target, never embedded in message text or logs.

Staged test scripts/test-halt-message-paths.cjs reviewed: static reads of two hard-coded sibling filenames via path.join(__dirname, rel) — no user input, no traversal, zero dependencies, no process execution. Safe.

Scanner inventory (honest): composer audit skipped (not applicable — Node project, no composer.json); PHPStan skipped (not applicable — Node project); npm audit RAN — initial 'npm audit --package-lock-only' failed ENOLOCK (package-lock.json gitignored, node_modules absent), fixed by generating an ephemeral lockfile via 'npm i --package-lock-only --ignore-scripts' (metadata-only, nothing installed), audit then reported 0 vulnerabilities (exit 0), ephemeral lockfile deleted afterwards; Snyk skipped (not configured — CLI not on PATH); SonarQube skipped (not configured — no sonar-project.properties, no ~/.keel/config/sonarqube.yml).

Findings: 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 1 LOW (repo-level: no committed lockfile means SCA is a point-in-time resolution, not reproducible pins — pre-existing release-flow trade-off, not introduced by this change), 1 INFO (pre-existing: instructional messages interpolate local .keel/state values into human-directed command suggestions; local-only, terminal output, no direct risk).

Blast radius: .keel/graph/codegraph.json absent — reverse-dependency query skipped; manual check confirms changed lines are message literals in self-contained CLI functions with no programmatic consumers.
CJIS: no law-enforcement data contact (presence flag: NO).
Memory writeback confirmed: .keel/memory/lessons.md contains L-1 (2026-07-09, KEEL-101) — defect-scope lesson present as required at this gate.
Verdict: PASS (0 HIGH) — report written to docs/security/KEEL-101-security-report.md.
decisions: Applied the scanner stack to the actual stack: composer audit and PHPStan recorded skipped (not applicable — Node project) instead of pretending PHP baselines ran.

Treated the initial npm audit ENOLOCK as fixable tooling rather than a FAILED gate: generated an ephemeral lockfile with --package-lock-only --ignore-scripts (no code installed, no scripts run), ran the audit for real (0 vulnerabilities), then deleted the lockfile to restore the tree — recorded the full method and its point-in-time caveat as a LOW finding instead of hiding it.

Rated the interpolated-state-values-in-instructional-messages pattern INFO not LOW/MEDIUM: it predates this diff, data is local-repo-controlled, and output is terminal/Slack text with no execution sink.
Did not block on the missing codegraph: blast-radius baseline is optional-when-present per spec; substituted a manual consumer check of the two changed message literals.
